title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Te Rau Hinengaro : The New Zealand Mental Health Survey: Summary Publication date: 2 September 2006 A summary of results from Te Rau Hinengaro: The New Zealand Mental Health Survey. Publication Mental health Surveys

## Te Rau Hinengaro : The New Zealand Mental Health Survey: Summary
A summary of results from Te Rau Hinengaro: The New Zealand Mental Health Survey.
- Te Rau Hinengaro : The New Zealand Mental Health Survey Publication date: 2 September 2006 Te Rau Hinengaro : The New Zealand Mental Health Survey provides information about the prevalence of mental disorders and their patterns of onset and impact for adults in New Zealand. Publication Mental health Surveys

## Te Rau Hinengaro : The New Zealand Mental Health Survey
Te Rau Hinengaro : The New Zealand Mental Health Survey provides information about the prevalence of mental disorders and their patterns of onset and impact for adults in New Zealand.
- Good Oral Health for All for Life Publication date: 2 August 2006 New Zealand has reached a turning point for oral health. Several decades of dramatic improvements in the oral health of young people have begun to reverse. Inequalities in oral health and in access to oral health services have become increasingly evident, with Māori, Pacific, rural and low socioeconomic populations all showing progressively poorer oral health relative to other groups. Publication Oral health

## Good Oral Health for All for Life
New Zealand has reached a turning point for oral health. Several decades of dramatic improvements in the oral health of young people have begun to reverse. Inequalities in oral health and in access to oral health services have become increasingly evident, with Māori, Pacific, rural and low socioeconomic populations all showing progressively poorer oral health relative to other groups.
- National Laboratory Guidelines for Pandemic Influenza Publication date: 2 June 2006 The National Laboratory Guidelines for Pandemic Influenza were compiled in consultation with members of the New Zealand Virology Laboratory Network. They are intended for use by health professionals and laboratory staff to ensure safe handling and collection of human specimens for diagnosis of influenza with pandemic potential. Publication Emergency management Influenza Pandemics

## National Laboratory Guidelines for Pandemic Influenza
The National Laboratory Guidelines for Pandemic Influenza were compiled in consultation with members of the New Zealand Virology Laboratory Network. They are intended for use by health professionals and laboratory staff to ensure safe handling and collection of human specimens for diagnosis of influenza with pandemic potential.
- Emergency management
- National Health Emergency Plan: Hazardous Substances Incident Hospital Guidelines Publication date: 2 December 2005 The Hazardous Substances Incident Hospital Guidelines are a part of the National Health Emergency Plan. Hazardous substances (Hazsub) incidents can occur anywhere in the community at any time, so health facilities, and in particular Emergency Departments, must have a level of capability for managing Hazsub incidents appropriate to their level of risk. Publication Emergency management

## National Health Emergency Plan: Hazardous Substances Incident Hospital Guidelines
The Hazardous Substances Incident Hospital Guidelines are a part of the National Health Emergency Plan. Hazardous substances (Hazsub) incidents can occur anywhere in the community at any time, so health facilities, and in particular Emergency Departments, must have a level of capability for managing Hazsub incidents appropriate to their level of risk.
- Emergency management
- Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT) Publication date: 1 March 2005 A review of New Zealand and overseas literature and of legislation to ascertain the effectiveness and safety of ECT and of the regulations surrounding the use of this treatment. Publication Mental health

## Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT)
A review of New Zealand and overseas literature and of legislation to ascertain the effectiveness and safety of ECT and of the regulations surrounding the use of this treatment.
- You Have Rights Publication date: 2 September 2004 Information to help you understand compulsory care and how it might affect you and your rights. Publication Mental health
Information to help you understand compulsory care and how it might affect you and your rights.
- Guidelines for Cultural Assessment: Māori Publication date: 2 August 2004 This guide enables the safety and skill development of Māori subject to the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003. Publication Disability Disability support Intellectual disabilities Māori health

## Guidelines for Cultural Assessment: Māori
This guide enables the safety and skill development of Māori subject to the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003.
- Intellectual disabilities
- Guidelines for the Role and Function of Compulsory Care Co-ordinators Publication date: 2 August 2004 These guidelines are intended to provide compulsory care co-ordinators (co-ordinators) and Regional Intellectual Disability Care Agencies (RIDCA) with information and guidance on the role of the co-ordinator under the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003 (the IDCCR Act). Publication Disability Disability support Intellectual disabilities

## Guidelines for the Role and Function of Compulsory Care Co-ordinators
These guidelines are intended to provide compulsory care co-ordinators (co-ordinators) and Regional Intellectual Disability Care Agencies (RIDCA) with information and guidance on the role of the co-ordinator under the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003 (the IDCCR Act).
- Intellectual disabilities
- A Guide to the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003 Publication date: 2 August 2004 These guidelines introduce key aspects of the application and operation of the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003 (‘the IDCCR Act’). They are intended for a wide audience including compulsory care co-ordinators, compulsory care managers, specialist assessors, disability service providers, caregivers, family and district inspectors. Publication Disability Disability support Intellectual disabilities Regulation

## A Guide to the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003
These guidelines introduce key aspects of the application and operation of the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003 (‘the IDCCR Act’). They are intended for a wide audience including compulsory care co-ordinators, compulsory care managers, specialist assessors, disability service providers, caregivers, family and district inspectors.
- Intellectual disabilities
- Guidelines for the Role and Function of Specialist Assessors Publication date: 2 August 2004 These guidelines are intended to provide specialist assessors designated under the Intellectual Disability (Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003 (IDCCR Act) with information and guidance on the role of the specialist assessor. Publication Disability Disability support Intellectual disabilities

## Guidelines for the Role and Function of Specialist Assessors
These guidelines are intended to provide specialist assessors designated under the Intellectual Disability (Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003 (IDCCR Act) with information and guidance on the role of the specialist assessor.
- Intellectual disabilities
- Guidelines for the Role and Function of Care Managers Publication date: 2 August 2004 These guidelines are intended to provide care managers with information and guidance on the role of the care manager under the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003 (the IDCCR Act). Publication Disability Disability support Intellectual disabilities

## Guidelines for the Role and Function of Care Managers
These guidelines are intended to provide care managers with information and guidance on the role of the care manager under the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003 (the IDCCR Act).

- A Health Equity Assessment Tool (Equity Lens) for Tackling Inequalities in Health Publication date: 2 May 2004 There is considerable evidence, both internationally and in New Zealand, of significant inequalities in health between socioeconomic groups, ethnic groups, people living in different geographical regions and males and females (Acheson 1998; Howden-Chapman and Tobias 2000). Publication Inequalities

## A Health Equity Assessment Tool (Equity Lens) for Tackling Inequalities in Health
There is considerable evidence, both internationally and in New Zealand, of significant inequalities in health between socioeconomic groups, ethnic groups, people living in different geographical regions and males and females (Acheson 1998; Howden-Chapman and Tobias 2000).
- NZ Food, NZ Children Publication date: 2 November 2003 It describes the methodologies and the data on nutrient intakes and their sources; food security; eating patterns; frequently eaten foods; physical activity patterns; dental health; anthropometric measures and selected nutrition-related clinical measures of New Zealand schoolchildren 5–14 years of age. Publication Child health Nutrition Statistics

## NZ Food, NZ Children
It describes the methodologies and the data on nutrient intakes and their sources; food security; eating patterns; frequently eaten foods; physical activity patterns; dental health; anthropometric measures and selected nutrition-related clinical measures of New Zealand schoolchildren 5–14 years of age.
- 2003 Direction of the Minister of Health Relating to Eligibility for Publicly Funded Personal Health and Disability Services Publication date: 2 October 2003 Pursuant to section 32 of the New Zealand Public Health and Disability Act 2000, the Minister of Health, after consulting with boards as required by that section, hereby gives the following direction to District Health Boards established under section 32 of that Act. Notice Health system

## 2003 Direction of the Minister of Health Relating to Eligibility for Publicly Funded Personal Health and Disability Services
Pursuant to section 32 of the New Zealand Public Health and Disability Act 2000, the Minister of Health, after consulting with boards as required by that section, hereby gives the following direction to District Health Boards established under section 32 of that Act.
